commit.message:
Fix humidity_index scale semantics in get_ams_units and get_ams_status docstrings

Veil-of-ignorance smoke test finding: naive agent saw humidity_index=5 on
both AMS units and recommended drying, because no scale context was provided.

The Bambu firmware scale is counterintuitive: 1=WET (alert), 5=DRY (good).
Without this documented in the tool docstrings, any agent reading the raw
value will invert the meaning and give incorrect advice.

Also documents that 0=unavailable/uninitialized and must not be treated as wet.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase 3 Step 9: First Triangle Demonstration - GPU command submission infrastructure

Implements the first GPU command submission demonstration binary, validating
the complete Phase 3 GPU submission infrastructure from Go to GPU.

## What Changed

### New Demonstration Binary
- Created cmd/gpu-triangle-demo/main.go (~430 LOC)
  - Demonstrates end-to-end GPU command submission workflow
  - GPU detection via render.DetectGPU()
  - GPU context creation via render.CreateContext()
  - Batch buffer allocation and command construction
  - Batch submission via render.SubmitBatch() with synchronous wait
  - GPU buffer presentation to X11 window via DRI3/Present extensions

### Go API Extensions
- Added BufferHandle.GemHandle() method to internal/render/dmabuf.go
  - Exposes GEM buffer handle for use in GPU command submission
  - Required for batch buffer and render target references

### Rust C ABI Extension
- Added buffer_get_handle() export in render-sys/src/lib.rs
  - Returns the GEM handle from a Buffer pointer
  - Called by Go's BufferHandle.GemHandle() via CGO

### Build System
- Updated Makefile with gpu-triangle-demo target
  - Uses same static linking configuration as other demos
  - Added to .PHONY targets list

## Phase 3 Achievement

This demo completes the GPU command submission infrastructure validation:
✅ GPU hardware detection (Gen9/Gen11/Gen12/Xe)
✅ GPU context creation (i915 and Xe drivers)
✅ Batch buffer allocation and construction
✅ GPU command submission with relocations
✅ Synchronous completion wait
✅ GPU buffer sharing with X11 display server

## Current Limitations (Phase 4 Scope)

The demo submits a minimal batch (MI_NOOP + MI_BATCH_BUFFER_END) as an
infrastructure smoke test. Full triangle rendering requires:
- Shader compilation (GLSL/WGSL → Intel EU binary) - Phase 4
- CPU→GPU buffer copy for batch data upload
- Render target surface state and pipeline configuration
- Vertex buffer setup and 3DPRIMITIVE command emission

The infrastructure is complete; the next phase will add the rendering pipeline.

## Validation Results

- All 100 Rust tests passing
- All 17 Go package tests passing
- Static linking verified: "not a dynamic executable"
- Zero regressions detected
- 4 complexity improvements in unrelated code (shm.HandleEvent, widget-demo)
- Binary builds successfully on x86_64 musl target

## Files Modified

- Makefile: Added gpu-triangle-demo build target
- PLAN.md: Marked Step 9 as complete with implementation notes
- cmd/gpu-triangle-demo/main.go: New demonstration binary (430 LOC)
- internal/render/dmabuf.go: Added GemHandle() method
- render-sys/src/lib.rs: Added buffer_get_handle() C ABI export
- baseline.json, post.json: Updated metrics (auto-generated)

Next: Step 10 - Integration Test Suite

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

CLI (main.rs):

- --explain: show recipe structure, steps, conditions

Parser (parser.rs):

commit.message:
feat: implement destructuring and spread support in bytecode generator

Destructuring support:
- Variable declarations: let {a, b} = obj; let [x, y] = arr;
- Catch parameters: catch ({message}) { ... }
- Assignment targets: [a, b] = expr; ({x, y} = expr)
- For-in loops: for (let {length} in obj) { ... }
- For-of loops: for (let [k, v] of entries) { ... }

All locations wire through the existing compile_binding_pattern() which
already handles object patterns, array patterns, nested patterns, and
default values.

Spread support:
- Array spread: [...arr, x, ...other] with dynamic index counter
- Object spread: {...obj, key: val} via new CopyDataProperties opcode
- Expr::Spread compiles its argument for use by parent contexts

New opcode: CopyDataProperties [target_reg, source_reg] — copies all
own enumerable properties from source to target PlainObject.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add setting to mute worker notifications in multi-agent groups (#295)

## Summary
Add a **Mute Worker Notifications** toggle under Settings →
Notifications that suppresses system notifications from worker sessions
in multi-agent groups. Only orchestrator and non-multi-agent session
notifications come through.

## Problem
When running multi-agent teams, every worker session completing its task
generates a notification, creating a flood of noise. Users want to
receive only the orchestrator notification (indicating the whole team
finished) rather than individual worker notifications.

## Changes
- **ConnectionSettings.cs** — added `MuteWorkerNotifications` property
(default `false`, backward-compatible)
- **SettingsRegistry.cs** — added UI toggle descriptor, only visible
when notifications are enabled
- **Settings.razor** — added mute toggle in the legacy notifications
section
- **CopilotService.Events.cs** — filter workers on `SessionIdleEvent`
notification path
- **CopilotService.Bridge.cs** — filter workers on `OnAttentionNeeded`
notification path (remote mode)
- **CopilotService.Organization.cs** — added
`IsWorkerInMultiAgentGroup()` helper with try/catch for background
thread safety

## Tests
- 15 new tests in `MuteWorkerNotificationsTests.cs` covering:
- Setting default value and JSON round-trip (including backward
compatibility)
  - Settings registry descriptor existence, visibility, and get/set
  - Worker identification in multi-agent groups
- Notification filtering: workers muted, orchestrators not muted,
non-multi-agent sessions not muted

commit.message:
perf: bounds-aware sub-region filter processing (#152)

* perf: bounds-aware sub-region filter processing

Extract the filter region as a sub-image before processing the
filter chain, so all primitives (blur, offset, flood, blend, merge,
drop-shadow) operate on significantly fewer pixels.

Key changes in Defs.applyFilter():
- computeProcessingRegion(): expands the filter region by blur
  sigma*3 + offset dx/dy to ensure no clipping
- extractSubRegion(): copies filter region pixels with
  System.arraycopy per row
- Filter buffer pool (buf1/buf2/buf3) sized to sub-region
- placeSubRegion(): copies filtered result back at offset
- feTile filter region adjusted to sub-region coordinates

For the benchmark SVG (400×300 canvas):
- 100×80 rect with blur σ=4: processes ~14K vs 120K pixels (8.4×)
- circle r=50 with blur σ=8: processes ~22K vs 120K pixels (5.3×)

Filters benchmark: 13.65ms → 7.69ms (44% faster, now 13% faster
than JSVG). Output is pixel-identical to previous version.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: feImage fragment rendering with sub-region offset

When processing filters in sub-region mode, feImage with fragment
references (href="#id") must translate the rendering context by
(-offsetX, -offsetY) so the referenced element renders at the
correct position within the sub-region buffer.

commit.message:
Add login rate limiting (10 attempts / 15 min per IP)

- Add LoginRateLimiter backed by dashmap for lock-free concurrent access
- Tracks failed attempts per IP; blocks after 10 failures in a 15-min window
- Resets on successful login; logs warnings when limit is reached
- Extracts real client IP from X-Forwarded-For (set by Cloud Run)
- Added dashmap = '6' to Cargo.toml
- CSRF note: not needed — Authorization: Bearer header cannot be sent
  cross-origin by browsers, making CSRF structurally impossible for this API
